prompt: Create a vintage Art Deco travel poster for Delhi, India, in a vertical 2:3 format. Use a warm aged-paper palette of saffron, ochre, sepia, burnt orange, and deep teal, with subtle film grain, distressed print texture, and a thin double-line border around the edges. The composition should have a huge bold serif headline at the top reading “DELHI” in dark teal, with widely spaced red-orange text beneath reading “INDIA”. Below the title on the left, place a simple lotus icon inside an arched outline, then the italic tagline “Where Empires Meet.”, a small decorative divider with a centered dot, and the coordinates “28.6139° N, 77.2090° E”. The right half and lower center should feature a monumental, photorealistic but posterized India Gate at golden hour, towering in perspective with detailed stone blocks, arch shadows, and an “INDIA” inscription near the top. In the hazy left background, show Humayun’s Tomb or a Mughal-style domed monument among trees, softened by sunset atmosphere. In the foreground, include exactly one cycle rickshaw with exactly two visible people: one cyclist pedaling and one seated passenger, rendered as dark silhouettes on a glowing road. Add small birds in the sky, long warm shadows, and sunlit haze behind the arch. Keep the design elegant, nostalgic, and tourism-focused, with no modern cars, no extra landmarks, and no additional text beyond the specified poster lettering.
